name: Spermidine induced histone hypoacetylation and longevity-linked autophagy
summary: Spermidine adds a cross-model histone acetylation signal, but the outcome is strongly mediated through autophagy.
statement: Spermidine extended lifespan across yeast, fly, and worm models, improved survival of human immune cells, inhibited oxidative stress in aging mice, and linked histone H3 hypoacetylation through histone acetyltransferase inhibition to autophagy induction.
population_or_model: Yeast, C. elegans, Drosophila, human immune cells, and aging mouse oxidative-stress assays
time_horizon: Model-organism lifespan and cellular survival assays
quantitative_note: The PubMed abstract describes marked lifespan extension across models but does not provide one unified effect size.
caveats: The most direct lifespan evidence is non-mammalian.
The aging-mouse signal is oxidative-stress suppression, not mammalian lifespan extension in this source.
Autophagy may be the dominant public interpretation rather than chromatin modulation alone.
